title: ranunculus nelsonii
intro: Ranunculus nelsonii, also known as Nelson's buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the western United States, known for its bright yellow flowers with five petals. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with applications in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus nelsonii can be made into salves, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting overall skin health.
